footer.tagline: Investment advisory and commercial real estate intermediation in Venezuela.

home.hero_headline: Access investment opportunities in Venezuela with professionals on the ground
home.hero_sub: T3 Advisors is the advisory firm that connects investors with the best investment opportunities in Venezuela, and sellers with the capital and support they need.
home.thesis_p1: Venezuela experienced one of the largest economic contractions in modern history: 80% between 2013 and 2021. Commercial assets trade today at 20-30% of their value from just a few years ago. Offices, hotels, industrial plants, agricultural land, and mining settlements are offered at prices that do not exist in any other Latin American market.
home.thesis_p2: Recovery has already begun, and sophisticated capital is looking toward Venezuela. The question is not whether Venezuela normalizes, but who positions themselves before it does.
home.services_sub: T3 Advisors manages the complete intermediation process, from asset preparation to transaction closing.
home.step1_desc: We advise sellers on what documentation is needed, transform scattered information into professional materials, and present each opportunity with the rigor an investor expects.
home.step2_desc: We identify and contact qualified buyers and investors. We filter serious prospects. Everything is handled under confidentiality agreements: the asset's identity is only revealed to committed counterparties.
home.step3_desc: We coordinate meetings between parties, facilitate negotiation of terms, organize the document flow during due diligence, and accompany every step until signing.
home.cta_portfolio_sub: Explore our portfolio of commercial assets, organized by sector and transaction type.
home.vertical_re_desc: Land, offices, retail spaces, residential complexes
home.team_desc: T3 Advisors operates with a team on the ground in Venezuela and professional communication capacity from Europe. We combine decades of experience in Venezuelan commercial real estate with the presentation and process standards that international capital demands.
home.cta_contact_sub: Let's talk. The first step is a conversation.
home.cta_invest_sub: Browse over 50 commercial real estate opportunities in Venezuela, organized by sector and transaction type. Full asset information is available upon signing an NDA.
home.cta_sell_sub: The first step is a conversation. T3 Advisors guides you through the process, organizes all asset documentation, and accompanies you from the first meeting through closing.
home.cta_services_sub: The first step is always a conversation. No commitments.

about.hero_p1: Venezuela has commercial assets trading at a fraction of their value, a commercial real estate market without professional intermediation, and millions of Venezuelans abroad with accumulated capital and a connection to the country. What it didn't have was a professional bridge between both worlds.
about.hero_p2: T3 Advisors was born to be that bridge. We open access to a market that is closed to outsiders and make it legible: structured information, professional documentation, and a clear process from first contact to closing.
about.team1_bio: Decades of experience in commercial real estate and tourism consulting in Venezuela. As director of IETASA and GPS Consultores, he led the financing of large-scale tourism projects and the structuring of commercial asset transactions. His contact network, built over more than 30 years, spans property owners, developers, hotel operators, investment funds, and government entities. That network is the reason T3 accesses opportunities that are not on the open market.
about.team2_bio: Economist with ten years of experience in strategic and data-driven implementation consulting, serving international clients in retail, banking, logistics, and financial services. A specialist in technology and artificial intelligence, he is capable of producing investment documentation at the standard institutional capital demands. His profile combines analytical rigor with technical capability, a combination rarely found in the Venezuelan market.
about.team3_bio: Economist specialized in strategy, business development, and sales operations, with extensive experience in technology startups and in the design of applied artificial intelligence systems. An expert in commercial operations, he can find the sales angle for any asset and communicate it in a serious, professional, and institutional manner. He can reach any domestic or international market and present an opportunity in a way that is credible in any context and before any type of investor.
about.model_p1: T3 Advisors acts as coordinator of each transaction. Our team produces the materials, identifies counterparties, and manages the process. But each specialty has its professional: lawyers for contracts, appraisers for valuations, auditors for financial reviews, experts for technical inspections.
about.model_p2: If the parties already have trusted professionals, we work with them. If not, we make our network available. In any case, T3 coordinates everyone's work so the transaction moves forward in an orderly manner.

placeholder.coming_soon_desc: We are preparing this page. In the meantime, contact us for any questions.

contact.subtitle: Tell us what you're looking for. We'll get back to you within 48 hours.

contact.success: Message received. We will contact you within 48 hours.
contact.success_buyer: Request received. You will receive an email with available opportunities in a few minutes.
contact.seller_tx_required: Please select at least one option.
contact.submit_error: An error occurred. Please try again.

For detailed information on any specific opportunity, you can request it directly through that email. You can also reach us directly through the contact details on the right.

whyVenezuela.hero_intro: Venezuela is experiencing one of the largest commercial asset dislocations in the Western Hemisphere. For some investors, that represents an unacceptable risk. For others, a once-in-a-generation window of opportunity. This page presents the data so you can decide.
whyVenezuela.context_headline: An economy that contracted 80% and is beginning to recover
whyVenezuela.context_p1: Between 2013 and 2021, Venezuela suffered the worst peacetime economic contraction in modern history. GDP fell approximately 80%. Industrial production collapsed. Private construction contracted 98% from its peak.
whyVenezuela.context_p2: Recovery began in 2022 (+3% GDP) and accelerated in 2024 (+5%), driven by increases in oil production and an industrial rebound of 16.8%. Manufacturing output reached 47.8% of installed capacity in the fourth quarter of 2024, its highest level since 2015 -- though that means more than half of industrial capacity remains idle.
whyVenezuela.context_p3: Venezuela is undergoing an institutional and economic transition. In January 2026, a new Hydrocarbons Law enabled international arbitration and private operational control in the oil industry. These regulatory changes, together with sustained signals of openness to foreign capital, create the most favorable environment for private investment since the oil nationalization of 1976.
whyVenezuela.context_p4: The economy operates de facto in dollars. Over 80% of commercial transactions are denominated in USD. For investors, this simplifies entry: you buy in dollars, you operate in dollars.
whyVenezuela.context_tl1_text: 80% GDP contraction — the worst peacetime decline in modern history
whyVenezuela.context_tl2_text: De facto dollarization — 80%+ of commercial transactions in USD
whyVenezuela.context_tl4_text: New Hydrocarbons Law, institutional transition and signs of economic opening
whyVenezuela.prices_headline: Assets at a fraction of their value
whyVenezuela.prices_p1: The former president of Venezuela's Real Estate Chamber put it this way: "If it costs between $1,000 and $1,500 to replace a square meter and it sells for $200-$300, it's selling at 20-30% of replacement value."
whyVenezuela.prices_p2: New construction is economically irrational at current prices: the cost of building ($1,000-$1,500/m² residential, $1,500-$2,500/m² commercial) exceeds market price by 3 to 5 times. This creates a natural price floor: new supply cannot compete until prices rise significantly.
whyVenezuela.prices_table_note: Sources: CBRE MarketView, El País, local market analysis. Caracas ranges reflect recent transactions and active market listings.
whyVenezuela.precedents_intro: Four markets went through comparable crises and then recovered. None is identical to Venezuela, but the patterns are instructive.
whyVenezuela.precedents_argentina_text: GDP fell 20% in three years. The peso collapsed from 1:1 to 3.9:1 against the dollar. Real estate prices in Buenos Aires dropped 30-40% in dollar terms. The recovery was sustained: 9% annual growth for five consecutive years. Those who bought during 2002-2003 captured total returns of 16-20% annually over a decade.
whyVenezuela.precedents_colombia_text: Medellin recorded zero years of decline between 2002 and 2024, with 7-8% annual appreciation accelerating to 10-12% in 2023-2024. Crime dropped 80%. By 2024, one in four properties sold was acquired by foreign buyers.
whyVenezuela.precedents_myanmar_text: Land prices tripled or quadrupled in Yangon suburbs in six months. Office rents reached Manhattan levels. But Myanmar is also the cautionary tale: by 2017-2018, rents fell 70% due to oversupply and political setbacks. The lesson: timing matters.
whyVenezuela.precedents_kurdistan_text: $20 billion was invested from 2006 onward, with $11.1 billion in housing alone. Prices multiplied fivefold in five years. Brands like Sheraton and Hilton entered the market. Kurdistan started from a worse base than Venezuela.
whyVenezuela.pullquote_text: An investor can't just fly to Caracas and start knocking on doors.
whyVenezuela.pullquote_attribution: Robert Koenigsberger, founder of Gramercy Funds ($7 billion in assets under management), January 2026
whyVenezuela.pullquote_note: Goldman Sachs, UBS, and Gramercy Funds have published dedicated analyses on Venezuela since January 2026, describing the market as a "tipping point" and "land of opportunity."
whyVenezuela.risks_intro: Investing in Venezuela requires conviction, local knowledge, and risk tolerance. Any informed investment decision requires understanding these factors.
whyVenezuela.risk1_text: The same party that expropriated over 1,400 companies now claims to welcome investors. Unpaid arbitration awards exceed $10 billion. The political transition is ongoing and its outcome is uncertain.
whyVenezuela.risk2_text: Venezuela ranks 178 out of 180 on the Transparency International index (score: 10/100). Property registries charge up to 40% of the sale value instead of the legally mandated 2%.
whyVenezuela.risk3_text: Approximately 200 power outages daily. Electricity rationing in 22 of 23 states. PDVSA pipelines not updated in 50 years.
whyVenezuela.risk4_text: The homicide rate is 26.8 per 100,000 inhabitants (2023), a significant improvement from peaks of 79-90 per 100,000 in 2013-2015, but still elevated by regional standards.
whyVenezuela.risk5_text: U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control restrictions affect transactions with a U.S. nexus. Each transaction requires a case-by-case compliance assessment.
whyVenezuela.risks_closing: We say this openly because we believe an informed investor is a better partner than one who discovers the problems after the fact.
whyVenezuela.cta_headline: The data is on the table. Want to see the opportunities?

investorsPage.hero_intro: Whether you are evaluating Venezuela for the first time or you know the market and want a professional counterparty, here is exactly how working with T3 Advisors works, what to expect at each step, and what support you have.
investorsPage.process_headline: How it works, step by step
investorsPage.process_sub: Seven clear stages, from the first evaluation through closing. At each one, you know what T3 does, what you need to bring, and what to expect.
investorsPage.step1_text: Fill out the contact form indicating which sectors or transaction types interest you. We send you a teaser with anonymous profiles of the opportunities: sector, general location, price range, and a summary. That is the starting point.
investorsPage.step2_text: Contact us indicating which opportunity or sector interests you. There is no commitment at this stage. It is a conversation.
investorsPage.step3_text: To protect the seller, the asset's identity and detailed information are only shared after signing a non-disclosure agreement (NDA). It is a standard document that protects both parties.
investorsPage.step4_text: Once the agreement is signed, you receive the confidential information memorandum: a detailed document with everything you need to evaluate the opportunity, including the sources behind every data point and the information gaps that exist.
investorsPage.step5_text: If you decide to move forward, we connect you with local professionals who know the terrain: transactional lawyers, auditors, appraisers, technical experts. You can use yours or ours. T3 coordinates the process.
investorsPage.step6_text: If the evaluation is positive, a letter of intent is formalized with the proposed terms: price, conditions, timelines. T3 facilitates the negotiation between the parties.
investorsPage.step7_text: A data room is organized for the formal review of the asset. T3 coordinates the document flow, follows up on every pending step, and accompanies the process through to the signing of the purchase agreement.
investorsPage.cta1_headline: Already know what you're looking for?
investorsPage.allies_headline: A network of experts by your side
investorsPage.allies_intro: Investing in Venezuela requires specialized support from people who understand the terrain. T3 Advisors works with a network of professionals experienced in major financial transactions. If you already have trusted advisors, we work with them. If not, we put our network at your disposal.
investorsPage.ally6_desc: Business plans, market research, and feasibility studies for major transactions
investorsPage.allies_closing: T3 recommends professionals, facilitates their engagement, and coordinates their work throughout the transaction. Each professional works directly for whoever retains them.
investorsPage.risks_text: Investing in Venezuela involves significant complexities: political risk, corruption, deteriorated infrastructure, sanctions restrictions (OFAC), and a regulatory environment in transition. T3 Advisors helps you understand and navigate each of these risks, and advises you on how to mitigate them with the best professionals in the market.
investorsPage.foreign_headline: How a foreign investor invests in Venezuela
investorsPage.foreign_intro: Venezuela is open to foreign investment and requires neither local partners nor special prior permits. What determines your starting point is your investor profile, particularly if you have ties to the United States. T3 guides you through each aspect and gives you access to the specialists who help you navigate these requirements.
investorsPage.reqs_us_1: OFAC compliance is mandatory. General licenses are self-executing but require active review before operating
investorsPage.reqs_us_2: No special prior permit from the Venezuelan government is required. The investment must be registered with BANCOEX within 60 days of the transaction
investorsPage.reqs_us_3: Partnering with a Venezuelan national is not required. Strategic sectors such as oil, mining, or telecoms may have special participation requirements
investorsPage.reqs_us_4: The investment vehicle can be incorporated outside Venezuela
investorsPage.reqs_us_5: International correspondent banking may cause operational delays
investorsPage.reqs_us_6: Regulatory framework is evolving rapidly. Up-to-date legal counsel is essential
investorsPage.reqs_eu_1: No sanctions restrictions equivalent to OFAC for most European investors
investorsPage.reqs_eu_2: No special prior permit from the Venezuelan government is required. The investment must be registered with BANCOEX within 60 days of the transaction
investorsPage.reqs_eu_3: Partnering with a Venezuelan national is not required. Strategic sectors such as oil, mining, or telecoms may have special participation requirements
investorsPage.reqs_eu_4: The investment vehicle can be incorporated outside Venezuela
investorsPage.reqs_eu_5: International correspondent banking may cause operational delays
investorsPage.reqs_eu_6: More straightforward process than for investors with U.S. ties
investorsPage.reqs_disclaimer: The regulatory framework, particularly U.S. sanctions (OFAC), is constantly evolving. This information is for guidance only and may not reflect the current state of regulations.
investorsPage.foreign_considerations_headline: Four considerations every transaction must keep in mind
investorsPage.foreign_ofac_badge: Applies if you have a U.S. nexus
investorsPage.foreign_vehicles_text: The standard practice is to incorporate the investment vehicle outside Venezuela, for legal certainty, banking access, and tax efficiency. Operating directly from Venezuela is also possible. T3 advises you on structuring your vehicle wherever it best suits your needs.
investorsPage.foreign_legal_text: Venezuela has bilateral investment treaties with 27 countries. The new Hydrocarbons Law (2026) enabled international arbitration of disputes, a fundamental shift for investor protection.
investorsPage.foreign_ofac_text: If you have a U.S. nexus (citizenship, residency, or a U.S.-registered company), every transaction in Venezuela requires a compliance assessment under OFAC regulations. T3 works with lawyers who specialize in this area.
investorsPage.foreign_repatriation_text: The economy operates de facto in dollars, which simplifies transactions. Capital repatriation considerations depend on the structure used and the investor's jurisdiction.
investorsPage.foreign_closing: T3 turns what seems complex into a clear process. None of these topics is an insurmountable obstacle. The difference is addressing it with the right advisor from the start.
investorsPage.cta2_headline: Have questions? The first step is a conversation.
investorsPage.cta2_sub: No commitment. Tell us what you're looking for and we'll point you in the right direction.

sellersPage.hero_intro: If you own an asset, an operating business, or a brand in Venezuela, and want to sell it, find an investor, or secure financing, here is exactly how we work: what we do, what we need from you, and what you can expect at each stage.
sellersPage.process_headline: How we take your transaction to closing
sellersPage.process_sub: Six clear stages, from the first conversation to closing. In each one, we explain what T3 does, what you need to contribute, and what to expect.
sellersPage.phase1_do:
We evaluate your asset and advise on the documentation needed
We build a structured, professional file
We define the commercial strategy: who we sell to, how we position it, and why someone would want to buy it
sellersPage.phase1_need:
Access to the asset and available financial information (it doesn't need to be perfect)
Legal ownership documents
Willingness to answer questions — you don't need to have everything ready from day one
sellersPage.phase1_expect: Within days, you will have a professional package that presents your asset at the level a serious investor expects to see
sellersPage.phase2_do:
We produce a teaser that describes the opportunity without revealing your asset's identity
We identify potential buyers based on the ideal profile and present the opportunity to them
sellersPage.phase2_need: Your authorization to present the asset anonymously
sellersPage.phase2_expect:
Your asset's identity is only shared after the buyer signs a confidentiality agreement
Everything is handled with absolute discretion
sellersPage.phase3_do:
We prepare a confidential information memorandum — a detailed, transparent document with sources behind every data point
Delivered only after the buyer signs an NDA
sellersPage.phase3_need:
Additional information that may arise during preparation
We organize and produce; you validate
sellersPage.phase3_expect:
Face-to-face meeting between you and the buyer to present the asset and answer questions
T3 moderates and facilitates the conversation
sellersPage.phase4_do:
We facilitate the letter of intent with proposed terms: price, payment conditions, timelines
We advise you on whether the terms are commercially reasonable
sellersPage.phase4_need: Your judgment on the terms — you decide, we advise
sellersPage.phase4_expect: Structured negotiation with legal involvement from both parties
sellersPage.phase5_do:
We organize a data room for the buyer and their advisors to review all documentation
We channel questions, coordinate responses, and keep the agreed timeline on track
sellersPage.phase5_need:
Cooperation with the buyer's information requests
Access for professionals who need to inspect the asset
Timely responses
sellersPage.phase5_expect:
The buyer will verify everything — this is normal and expected
T3 anticipates the questions and helps you have the answers ready in advance
sellersPage.phase6_do:
We facilitate the final negotiation of terms between the parties
We coordinate the timeline toward signing
We are present at closing
sellersPage.phase6_need: Willingness to negotiate in good faith
sellersPage.phase6_expect:
Both parties' lawyers draft and review the contract
T3 ensures the agreed terms are accurately reflected and the process reaches signing in an orderly manner
sellersPage.fee_headline: We charge only when the transaction closes successfully.
sellersPage.fee_body: T3 Advisors charges a percentage of the final transaction amount, agreed upon at the start of the process. No upfront fees, no retainers.
sellersPage.pullquote_text: T3 coordinates the parties so the transaction reaches completion
sellersPage.why1_text: In Venezuela, there is no organized market where you can list an asset and have buyers come to you. Opportunities move in closed circles. We give you visibility with buyers and investors you cannot reach on your own.
sellersPage.why2_text: The difference between an asset that gets taken seriously and one that gets ignored is how it is presented. A buyer who evaluates opportunities professionally expects materials at that level: organized information, verified numbers, documentation in order. We produce that.
sellersPage.why3_text: In a market where word travels fast, discretion matters. We handle the entire operation under confidentiality agreements. Every step has a clear structure: what is shared, when, with whom, under what conditions. You know what to expect at all times.
sellersPage.why4_text: We work with over 50 opportunities across different sectors in Venezuela. That gives us accumulated knowledge that benefits every seller: we know what buyers are looking for, what concerns them, what holds them back, and what convinces them.
sellersPage.faq1_q: What do I need to get started?
sellersPage.faq1_a: A conversation. Tell us about the asset, we advise you on what documents we need, and we start building the file from there. You don't need to have everything ready from day one; we move forward together.
sellersPage.faq2_a: A percentage of the transaction value, defined on a case-by-case basis when we formalize the engagement. We only charge if the deal closes.
sellersPage.faq3_a: It depends on the asset and the state of the documentation. We complete material preparation in days. Finding the right buyer and bringing the negotiation to close has its own rhythm, but T3 keeps the process in constant motion.
sellersPage.faq4_a: No, and be wary of anyone who does. What we do guarantee is that your asset will be presented in the best possible way, in front of the right counterparties, with a serious process. That maximizes the chances, but the final decision is the buyer's.
sellersPage.faq5_a: We don't charge. T3 works on a success-fee model: we only earn fees if the transaction closes. That means we take on the risk with you, and our incentive is to bring your deal to a successful close.
sellersPage.faq6_q: I don't want to sell, I want to find a partner or investor
sellersPage.faq6_a: We do that too. The process shares the same structure: we prepare the project documentation, identify investors with the right profile, and accompany the negotiation. What changes is the focus: instead of transferring ownership, we are presenting an investment opportunity. T3 has experience in both types of transactions.
sellersPage.cta_headline: Have an asset you want to sell or seek investment for?
sellersPage.cta_sub: The first step is a no-commitment conversation.

portfolioPage.hero_intro_1: T3 Advisors identifies, analyzes, and structures investment opportunities across six key sectors of the Venezuelan economy. Each sector represents a specific investment thesis, backed by real assets in our pipeline.
portfolioPage.hero_intro_2: For each sector, you can request the complete list of available opportunities through the contact form. You will receive preliminary information on all of them directly to your email. Access to detailed information on each opportunity is managed under a confidentiality agreement.
portfolioPage.sector_title_re: Commercial assets at a fraction of replacement cost
portfolioPage.sector_p1_re: Venezuela's prime commercial real estate (office towers, shopping centers, residential complexes) can be acquired at 10% to 30% of replacement cost. Caracas, Valencia, Maracaibo, and Barquisimeto maintain established commercial corridors with proven demand.
portfolioPage.sector_p2_re: The recovery cycle creates a unique window: properties with active rental income and existing infrastructure, priced well below construction costs and regional comparables.
portfolioPage.sector_title_hos: 2,700 km of Caribbean coast, a sector to rediscover
portfolioPage.sector_p1_hos: Venezuela has Caribbean coastline, Andean mountains, and national parks that rival the best in the region. Its hotel and tourism infrastructure exists but has been severely undervalued for over a decade.
portfolioPage.sector_p2_hos: Hotels, resorts, and apart-hotels with active operations and recovering occupancy represent an unusual entry point into a market with massive upside as travel routes normalize.
portfolioPage.sector_title_ag: Fertile land, existing infrastructure, unmet demand
portfolioPage.sector_p1_ag: Venezuela has some of South America's most productive agricultural land. Coffee, cacao, cattle ranching, and diversified agriculture have deep roots in the country and an export tradition that predates oil.
portfolioPage.sector_p2_ag: Processing plants, farms, and export-ready operations with existing infrastructure are available at prices that reflect years of underinvestment, not the land's actual productive capacity.
portfolioPage.sector_title_ind: Underutilized installed capacity in strategic corridors
portfolioPage.sector_p1_ind: Venezuela's industrial base, concentrated in the Valencia-Maracay corridor and the Guayana region, operates well below capacity. Manufacturing plants with installed equipment and established supply chains are available.
portfolioPage.sector_p2_ind: Strategic positioning for export to the Caribbean and Latin America, combined with existing infrastructure and skilled workforce, creates compelling entry points for both operators and investors.
portfolioPage.sector_title_hc: Private medical infrastructure with growing demand
portfolioPage.sector_p1_hc: Venezuela's private healthcare sector has maintained quality standards amid economic challenges. Clinics and medical centers serve growing demand as the public system reorganizes.
portfolioPage.sector_p2_hc: Facilities with updated equipment, established patient bases, and specialized capabilities represent an essential sector with stable fundamentals and significant expansion potential.
portfolioPage.sector_title_min: World-class mineral reserves at an early stage
portfolioPage.sector_p1_min: Venezuela holds some of the world's largest reserves of gold, diamonds, bauxite, iron, and coltan, concentrated in the Guayana region south of the Orinoco.
portfolioPage.sector_p2_min: Formal concessions with completed geological studies, environmental permits, and existing camp infrastructure offer a structured entry point into one of the last major untapped mining regions in Latin America.
portfolioPage.cta_sub: Detailed opportunities are shared under a confidentiality agreement. Request access and a T3 advisor will contact you.
portfolioPage.nda_note: Full details under NDA · request access

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains how T3 Advisors collects, uses and protects your personal information when you visit our website or contact us. By using the site, you accept the practices described here.

sections.title: Data controller
paragraphs: T3 Advisors, based in Caracas, Venezuela, is the data controller responsible for the personal data collected through this site. For any inquiry about your personal data, please contact us at alejandro.tovar@t3-advisors.com.

sections.title: What data we collect
paragraphs: When you fill out the contact form we collect the following data:
bullets: Full name.
Email address.
Company (optional).
Phone number (optional).
Information about your interest: sectors, transaction type, asset type, location and price range.
Free-text message (optional).
IP address, used only to apply rate limits and prevent abuse of the form.
Language preference, stored in a functional cookie.

sections.title: How we use your data
paragraphs: We use your information to respond to your request and maintain the business conversation, send you information about investment opportunities that match your profile, keep an internal record of leads for follow-up, and comply with legal obligations when applicable.
The legal basis is the performance of a contract or pre-contractual measures at your request, or our legitimate interest in managing the business, depending on the specific case.

sections.title: How long we keep it
bullets: Form data: while an active business relationship exists and up to 3 years after the last contact, unless a longer retention period is required by law.
IP records for rate limiting: no longer than 24 hours.

sections.title: Who we share it with
paragraphs: We do not sell your data or share it for advertising purposes. We only share it with third parties that provide services necessary for the operation of the site and our business, and that act as data processors on our behalf. These categories are:
bullets: Website infrastructure and hosting providers.
Transactional email delivery providers.
Tools for internal management and recording of business contacts.
Public authorities or bodies when required by a legal obligation.

sections.title: International transfers
paragraphs: Some of our providers are based outside Venezuela, mainly in the United States and the European Union. In those cases, your data may be transferred outside your country of residence. These providers rely on standard protection mechanisms, such as Standard Contractual Clauses or other internationally recognised frameworks.

sections.title: Your rights
paragraphs: You may exercise the following rights at any time:
bullets: Access the personal data we hold about you.
Request rectification of inaccurate data.
Request deletion of your data when it is no longer necessary.
Object to processing or request its restriction.
Request portability of your data.
Withdraw consent at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of prior processing.

sections.title: How to exercise your rights
paragraphs: To exercise any of the rights above, please contact us at alejandro.tovar@t3-advisors.com. We will respond within a maximum of 30 days. If you believe we have not handled your request properly, you may file a complaint with the data protection authority competent in your jurisdiction.

sections.title: Security
paragraphs: We apply reasonable technical and organisational measures to protect your data: restricted access, encrypted transmission via HTTPS and TLS, and providers with recognised certifications. No transmission over the internet is 100% secure, but we make our best effort to protect it.

sections.title: Cookies
paragraphs: The site uses a single functional cookie (NEXT_LOCALE) to remember your language preference. We do not use advertising, analytics or third-party tracking cookies. See our Cookie Policy for details.

sections.title: Changes to this policy
paragraphs: We may update this policy from time to time. The current version will always be the one published on this site.

sections.title: Contact
paragraphs: For any question about this policy or about how we handle your data, please write to alejandro.tovar@t3-advisors.com. T3 Advisors, Caracas, Venezuela.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: This page explains which cookies the T3 Advisors website uses, what they do, and how you can control them.

sections.title: What cookies are
paragraphs: Cookies are small text files that a website stores in your browser. They allow the site to recognise your browser on later visits and remember certain preferences.

sections.title: Cookies this site uses
paragraphs: We only use strictly necessary or functional cookies:
bullets: NEXT_LOCALE: remembers the language (Spanish or English) you explicitly selected in the menu switcher. Duration: up to 1 year. Not shared with third parties.

sections.title: Cookies we do not use
paragraphs: We do not use analytics, advertising, social media or tracking cookies. We do not install Meta, Google Ads or similar marketing pixels. We do not load third-party marketing scripts.

sections.title: Technical infrastructure cookies
paragraphs: Vercel, the provider that hosts the site, may set temporary technical cookies needed for the site to operate, such as load balancing or deployment previews. These are session cookies and are not used for commercial purposes.

sections.title: How to control them
paragraphs: You can accept, block or delete cookies from your browser settings. If you block the NEXT_LOCALE cookie, the site will not remember your language preference between sessions, but will continue to work correctly.

sections.title: Changes
paragraphs: If in the future we add analytics or other non-essential cookies, we will implement a prior consent banner before activating them. This page will be updated accordingly.

sections.title: Contact
paragraphs: If you have questions about this cookie policy, please write to alejandro.tovar@t3-advisors.com.

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.intro: The following terms govern the use of the T3 Advisors website and your interaction with our services. By browsing the site or contacting us, you accept these terms.

sections.title: About T3 Advisors
paragraphs: T3 Advisors is a professional advisory practice in investment and commercial real estate, based in Caracas, Venezuela. We connect investors with investment opportunities in Venezuela and guide asset owners through sale processes or the search for capital.

sections.title: Purpose of the site
paragraphs: This site is for informational purposes only. The content of the site does not, under any circumstances, constitute:
bullets: A public or private offer of securities or financial instruments.
A personalised investment recommendation.
Legal, accounting, tax or financial advice.

sections.title: Portfolio information
paragraphs: Information about investment opportunities is shared only under confidentiality, after a formal contact process and the signing of the relevant agreements. Any opportunity profiles that T3 Advisors shares upon request are anonymous and indicative; any investment decision requires a full due diligence process, which is the sole responsibility of the investor with the support of independent professional advisors.

sections.title: Risk
paragraphs: Investing in emerging markets, including Venezuela, involves material risks and potential total loss of capital. You acknowledge these risks and assume their management. Risk factors include, without limitation:
bullets: Regulatory change and public policy shifts.
Political instability.
International sanctions, including OFAC.
Currency controls and foreign-exchange risk.
Limited liquidity and uncertain exit horizons.
Weakness of contract enforcement mechanisms.

sections.title: No guarantee of results
paragraphs: T3 Advisors does not guarantee returns, execution timelines, or the successful closing of any transaction. Precedents or cases mentioned on the site are illustrative and do not constitute any promise of future performance.

sections.title: Intellectual property
paragraphs: All content on the site (text, logos, images, structure) is the property of T3 Advisors or used under licence. Reproduction, adaptation, distribution or commercial use without written authorisation is not permitted.

sections.title: Third-party links
paragraphs: The site may contain links to third-party pages. T3 Advisors does not control and is not responsible for the content, privacy policies or practices of those sites.

sections.title: Limitation of liability
paragraphs: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, T3 Advisors will not be liable for indirect, incidental or consequential damages, loss of profits or loss of opportunity arising from the use of the site or from decisions taken based on its content.

sections.title: Governing law and arbitration
paragraphs: These terms are governed by the laws of the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela. Any dispute arising out of or in connection with these terms or the services provided by T3 Advisors shall be finally resolved by international arbitration, administered by the Centro Empresarial de Conciliación y Arbitraje (CEDCA) of Caracas or, at the election of the parties, by the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC). The arbitration shall be conducted in Spanish, by a sole arbitrator, and the award shall be final and binding on the parties.

sections.title: Changes
paragraphs: We may update these terms at any time. The current version is always the one published on the site.

sections.title: Contact
paragraphs: For any question about these terms, please write to alejandro.tovar@t3-advisors.com. T3 Advisors, Caracas, Venezuela.
